title: stuckenia filiformis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Stuckenia filiformis subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Water Stirk or Western Stirk, is a type of aquatic plant species belonging to the family Potamogetonaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Stuckenia filiformis subsp. occidentalis are believed to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its dried leaves and stems, which can be decocted in water to create a tea or infused in oil to create a topical remedy.
